title: A Lamp Against the Fog
content: The fog came early to Tingen that autumn, settling over the cobblestones like a grey tide. Mr. Klein walked the length of Daly Street with his collar turned up against the damp. He had learned, in his short months among the Nighthawks, that the city kept its secrets in the hours before dawn. A gas lamp guttered at the corner, throwing his shadow long and thin across the wet stone. Somewhere behind the mist a cart wheel groaned, then fell silent. He paused at the iron railing of the canal and listened. The water below was black and patient, and it told him nothing.

title: A Lamp Against the Fog
content: He paused at the iron railing of the canal and listened. The water below was black and patient, and it told him nothing. Still, the spirit world pressed close here, thinner than paper, and a Seer learned to feel that pressure the way other men felt the cold. He drew the small copper coin from his pocket and let it rest in his palm. Heads, he would cross the bridge; tails, he would turn back toward the church. The coin was a habit more than a method, but habits, he had found, kept a man from thinking too hard about the dark. It landed heads. Klein smiled despite himself and stepped onto the bridge, the fog swallowing him whole.

title: The Tarot Gathering
content: Above the grey fog, in a space that was not quite a room, the Tarot Club came to order. The Fool sat at the head of a long table that stretched into the mist on either side, and the other members took their seats one by one. The High Priestess arrived first, calm as still water, her presence soft and certain. The Hanged Man followed, all easy laughter and quick eyes, the sort of man who counted exits out of habit. They spoke of small things at first, of weather and rumor, the way conspirators do when they are not yet sure of one another. Then the Fool raised one hand, and the table fell quiet.

title: The Tarot Gathering
content: They spoke of small things at first, of weather and rumor, the way conspirators do when they are not yet sure of one another. Then the Fool raised one hand, and the table fell quiet. There was business to discuss, he told them, a matter of a stolen relic and a buyer who paid in secrets rather than coin. The members leaned in, and the fog leaned in with them.

title: Sequence 9: Seer
content: The Seer is the Sequence 9 Beyonder of the Fool pathway. A Seer gains rudimentary divination abilities, including augury, dream interpretation, and the ability to sense spirits at close range. The potion formula for the Seer requires the main ingredient and a set of supplementary materials prepared under specific conditions. Newly advanced Seers are advised to digest the potion fully through acting before attempting any further advancement. Failure to control the corresponding emotions and desires may lead to loss of control, a fate the pathway's lore treats with grave seriousness.
